rationale: The plan extends a single interface and function in one file (src/input/signals.ts) with 4 new optional fields using null fallbacks, consistent with the existing devicePixelRatio pattern. No new files are created, no new dependencies are introduced, no storage or network APIs are used, and the change is additive-only. The existing BrowserSignals interface is not consumed by any downstream modules yet (US-004 is early in the dependency chain), so the interface extension has zero breaking impact. All new reads use standard, synchronous, permission-free browser APIs (navigator.hardwareConcurrency, window.matchMedia, ontouchstart/maxTouchPoints). The testing strategy correctly identifies the jsdom/matchMedia mock requirement and plans to update the exact-keys assertion before adding new tests.

architectural_impact.public_api_surface_change: additive — 4 new optional fields on BrowserSignals

conditions: matchMedia calls must be wrapped in try/catch as specified, since jsdom and some older environments lack it
The exact-keys test (line 66-70 of tests/signals.test.ts) must be updated in the same commit as the interface change to avoid transient test failures
notes: Clean, minimal-footprint change that follows established patterns in the codebase. The null-fallback convention for unavailable APIs is already proven by devicePixelRatio. No architectural concerns.
